napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.0.event: Born as the second son among eight children in Ajaccio on the island of Corsica.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.1.event: Traveled to France with his older brother Joseph and enrolled in the religious school attached to the Autun Abbey.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.2.event: Enrolled in the military school at Brienne.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.3.event: Enrolled in the École Militaire in Paris.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.4.event: His father, Carlo, passed away from stomach cancer.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.5.event: Passed the artillery officer examination and was commissioned as a second lieutenant at the age of 16.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.6.event: Assigned as an artillery officer to the La Fère regiment stationed in Valence.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.7.event: Served in Auxonne after the La Fère regiment relocated its garrison there.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.9.event: Obtained leave and returned to his hometown in Corsica.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.11.event: Detained by Paoli's faction, had his home and farm looted, and subsequently fled Corsica with his family.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.12.event: Recaptured the L'Eguillette and Balaguier forts in the Siege of Toulon, achieving victory and being promoted to brigadier general.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.13.event: The Thermidorian Reaction occurred, leading to the execution of Robespierre and his associates.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.14.event: Imprisoned due to his friendship with Robespierre's younger brother, Augustin, but was soon released.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.15.event: Suppressed the royalist uprising in Paris.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.17.event: Appointed as the commander of the Army of Italy.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.18.event: Occupied the Austrian capital, Vienna, and signed the Treaty of Campo Formio.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.19.event: Began the Egyptian campaign, landing at the port of Alexandria with over 50,000 troops.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.20.event: The French navy was defeated by Horatio Nelson's British fleet at the Battle of Aboukir Bay.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.21.event: Escaped from Egypt and returned to France.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.22.event: Staged the Coup of 18 Brumaire along with Emmanuel Joseph Sieyès, a powerful figure in the revolutionary government.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.23.event: A new constitution establishing three consuls was put to a national referendum and passed.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.24.event: The French expeditionary force crossed the Great St Bernard Pass.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.25.event: Achieved victory over the Austrian army at the Battle of Marengo.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.26.event: Established the Bank of France to promote economic stability.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.27.event: Amicably resolved relations with the United States through the Treaty of Mortefontaine.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.28.event: Signed the Concordat with Pope Pius VII.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.29.event: Dispatched an expeditionary force of over 30,000 men to Haiti (Saint-Domingue) in an attempt to recapture the colony and restore slavery.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.30.event: Signed the Treaty of Amiens with Britain, bringing stability for the first time in 10 years.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.32.event: Became First Consul for Life, solidifying his dictatorial power.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.33.event: Sold Louisiana to the United States for 15 million dollars.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.34.event: An assassination plot against Napoleon by Georges Cadoudal and royalists hoping for a Bourbon restoration was uncovered.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.35.event: Abandoned the occupation of Haiti and withdrew his troops.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.36.event: Haiti formally gained independence, becoming the first black republic.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.37.event: The acceptance of the empire was decided through a national referendum.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.38.event: Crowned Emperor Napoleon I of the First French Empire in a coronation ceremony at Notre-Dame Cathedral.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.39.event: The French navy suffered a crushing defeat against the British navy led by Nelson at the Battle of Trafalgar.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.40.event: The French army achieved a decisive victory at the Battle of Austerlitz, leading Austria to surrender by signing the Treaty of Pressburg.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.41.event: Installed his brother Louis as the King of Holland.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.42.event: Defeated the Prussian army at the Battle of Jena–Auerstedt and entered Berlin.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.43.event: Issued the Berlin Decree, establishing the Continental System.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.44.event: Signed the Treaties of Tilsit with Alexander I of Russia.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.45.event: Invaded Portugal after it defied the Continental System.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.47.event: The residents of Madrid launched an uprising against the French troops.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.49.event: When the Pope issued an excommunication, Napoleon had him arrested and confined in Savona.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.51.event: Remarried Marie Louise, daughter of Emperor Francis I of Austria.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.52.event: Sweden offered the position of heir to the Swedish throne to French Marshal Bernadotte.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.53.event: Marie Louise gave birth to Napoleon II, the King of Rome.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.54.event: Launched the Russian campaign, leading a Grand Armee of 600,000 men.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.55.event: Forcibly relocated the Pope to Fontainebleau.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.56.event: The French army emerged victorious in the major Battle of Borodino.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.58.event: The French army retreated from Russia.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.59.event: Signed the Concordat of Fontainebleau with Pope Pius VII.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.60.event: Suffered a major defeat at the Battle of Leipzig after being besieged by the allied forces.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.62.event: Signed the Treaty of Fontainebleau and was exiled as the sovereign of the island of Elba.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.63.event: Escaped from the island of Elba.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.64.event: Entered Paris and was restored as the Emperor of the French.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.65.event: Defeated at the Battle of Waterloo by the combined British and Prussian forces, marking the end of the Hundred Days.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.66.event: Exiled to Saint Helena in the South Atlantic.
napoleon-bonaparte.timeline.68.event: His remains were returned to France and interred in Les Invalides in Paris.
napoleon-bonaparte.keyAchievements.0.des: In 1804, he enacted the French Civil Code, known as the Napoleonic Code, which greatly influenced civil law worldwide.
napoleon-bonaparte.keyAchievements.1.tit: Advancement of Military Tactics and Systems
napoleon-bonaparte.keyAchievements.1.des: He influenced modern warfare militarily and advanced tactics, strategy, training, organization, logistics, uniforms, and the system of awards.
napoleon-bonaparte.keyAchievements.2.tit: Victory at the Siege of Toulon
napoleon-bonaparte.keyAchievements.2.des: After leading the victory at the Siege of Toulon in 1793, he was promoted to brigadier general at the age of 24.
napoleon-bonaparte.keyAchievements.3.des: He achieved great success in the Italian campaign in 1796, winning a series of victories in the War of the First Coalition and enjoying a triumphant military career.
napoleon-bonaparte.keyAchievements.4.tit: Establishment of the Bank of France
napoleon-bonaparte.keyAchievements.4.des: He established the Bank of France in 1800 to promote economic stability.
napoleon-bonaparte.keyAchievements.5.tit: Institution of the Legion of Honour
napoleon-bonaparte.keyAchievements.5.des: In 1802, he instituted the renowned Legion of Honour.
